title: Issue tree builder
prompt: Break the problem into three clean branches.
problemStatement: Why is the Olympics losing its cultural impact?

sampleAnswer.hypothesis: The Olympics is losing gravity because of broken culture, a lost story arc, and lack of legitimate transparency.

arguments.title: Shared ritual is breaking
supportingDetails: Fragmented attention: no single shared moment; audiences are split across platforms, time zones, and competing "world stages."
Broadcast-first packaging: still feels like a TV event, while audiences now consume culturally through feeds and communities.
Highlights over attendance: people "catch up" later, so the Games lose the live, collective timestamp feeling.

arguments.title: Meaning is getting replaced by moment-chasing
supportingDetails: Clip culture: short-form rewards shock, controversy, and spectacle; meaning needs arcs and buildup.
Moment-chasing storytelling: highlights/virality outshine training, stakes, and excellence — so "sacredness" doesn't accumulate.
Winners aren't legible: without consistent athlete-led arcs, audiences don't feel the personal stakes behind excellence.

arguments.title: Legitimacy and tone are getting noisier
supportingDetails: Trust volatility: politics and polarization make the Olympics feel biased or contested depending on the viewer.
Sensitivity: generic "unity" messaging can land as tone-deaf in a fractured world.
Over-commercialization signal: sponsorship/ad load makes the Games feel monetized (less "earned," more "sold").
